summary:
- Airbus prioritized customer needs over European identity, even at the expense of alienating European partners.  
- The project succeeded because it was the least European European project ever, prioritizing product quality and customer service over political symbolism.  
- The company de-emphasized its European identity to appeal to US airlines, while Boeing's decline created a market opening.  
- The result is a 40-year success story built on putting the customer first, even if that meant snubbing European suppliers and governments.
